data.text: All six child beads exist and are currently open. This is a decomposed epic bead that tracks the completion of its six children. Since the acceptance criterion is "All six child beads closed," I'll create a verification command that the orchestrator can use to validate when all children are complete.

input.content:
status: open
reason: awaiting completion of child beads B1-B6
verification_command: bash -c 'for bid in ddx-fbfcbd17 ddx-e8956709 ddx-018da544 ddx-fe2a5722 ddx-94ff4f5d ddx-4eb047cc; do status=$(ddx bead show "$bid" --json 2>/dev/null | jq -r .Status); [ "$status" = "closed" ] || exit 1; done'

ID:       ddx-fbfcbd17
Title:    doc: declare reconcile-close vs evidence-close in TD-031
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 0
Labels:   phase:6, area:bead, area:lifecycle, area:docs, kind:doc, refactor
Parent:   ddx-6e6d08f8
Desc:     PROBLEM
The bead lifecycle spec (referenced as TD-031) must formally declare the distinction between reconcile-close and evidence-close to match the implementation.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/reconcile.go:239-243 documents via code comment that 'reconcile-close is a meta-close that intentionally bypasses ClosureGate', but this distinction is not reflected in the formal specification document. The spec exists as a reference in types.go:78 ("TD-031 §2") but the full document is not in this repo.

PROPOSED FIX
Create or amend TD-031 (or equivalent specification document in docs/helix/03-design/) to:
1. Define reconcile-close: meta-close via bead.Reconcile when all transitive dependencies are closed, bypasses ClosureGate because evidence is inherited by reference through dependency edges.
2. Define evidence-close: direct close via Store.Close, requires closing_commit_sha evidence from execution session.
3. Document when each is used: reconcile-close for dependency satisfaction, evidence-close for work-completion evidence.
4. Reference both concepts from the lifecycle transition documentation.

data.text:
This decomposed epic has been properly set up with a verification command. All six child beads are created and listed in the notes. The verification command checks that all child beads transition to `closed` status, which will satisfy the acceptance criterion "All six child beads (B1-B6) closed."

The orchestrator will execute the child beads in dependency order through normal queue drainage, and the verification command will permit closure of this parent bead once all children complete.
